title: rhus glabra f. laciniata
intro: Rhus glabra f. laciniata, also known as the cut-leaf sumac, is a deciduous shrub or small tree species in the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its compound leaves with deeply cut or lobed foliage. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rhus glabra f. laciniata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, making it a valuable herbal remedy. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, while its resinous sap is used to create ointments and salves.
